codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly adds all four unified envelope fields (version, routing_backend, routing_reason, sidecar_used) to every JSON output path in the Rust binary. Changes span main.rs (RoutingMetadata, SearchResultJson, new ApplyVerifyJson struct, normalize_gpu_sidecar_json function, emit_json_metadata), backend_ast.rs (RewritePlan struct with new fields), and index.rs (clippy fix + type alias). Test coverage is thorough across all JSON output paths: CPU search, index search, AST search, rewrite plan, apply+verify, and GPU sidecar. The worker followed TDD correctly (wrote failing test first), ran all local gates (cargo test, clippy, ruff, mypy, pytest), and verified the release binary against real data.

issues.description: Version field hardcoded as `version: 1` in plan_rewrites() instead of using the JSON_OUTPUT_VERSION constant defined in main.rs. Same issue at line 411 in plan_and_apply(). If the version constant is bumped in main.rs, backend_ast.rs would be stale. Consider exporting the constant from a shared location or passing it as a parameter.

normalize_gpu_sidecar_json inserts envelope fields at the end of the serde_json::Map, while typed structs (SearchResultJson, ApplyVerifyJson) serialize envelope fields first. This produces inconsistent field ordering across JSON output paths. Cosmetic only — JSON spec does not define object key ordering — but may confuse consumers that naively compare outputs.

The FileTrigramHits type alias and .is_some_and() clippy fixes are unrelated to the envelope feature but bundled in the same commit. These are correct and helpful but would ideally be in a separate commit for traceability.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md 'User Preferences' section mentions 'Unified JSON envelope: every machine-readable output must include version, routing_backend, routing_reason' but omits sidecar_used from the list. The implementation correctly includes all four fields, but AGENTS.md should list sidecar_used explicitly to prevent future workers from omitting it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md last line: 'Unified JSON envelope: every machine-readable output must include version, routing_backend, routing_reason' — missing sidecar_used. Compare to architecture.md which correctly lists all four: 'version (u32), routing_backend (string), routing_reason (string), sidecar_used (bool)'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The JSON_OUTPUT_VERSION constant lives in main.rs but backend_ast.rs hardcodes `version: 1` without referencing it. If version is ever bumped, there is no compile-time safeguard to keep backend_ast.rs in sync. Consider documenting this coupling risk or moving the constant to a shared module.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: rust_core/src/main.rs:16 defines `const JSON_OUTPUT_VERSION: u32 = 1;`. rust_core/src/backend_ast.rs:340 and :411 both use `version: 1` literal.

summary: The unify-json-envelope feature passes review. All four envelope fields (version, routing_backend, routing_reason, sidecar_used) are correctly added to every JSON output path: CPU/AST search metadata, index search JSON, rewrite plan, apply+verify wrapper, and GPU sidecar output. The implementation is clean, test coverage is comprehensive across all paths, and the worker followed TDD and ran all required local gates. Three non-blocking issues were noted: hardcoded version in backend_ast.rs (maintenance risk), inconsistent field ordering in GPU sidecar JSON, and unrelated clippy fixes bundled in the same commit.
